title: Will the US agree to a new Iranian nuclear deal this year?

current.lean_note: no +EV value bet at the ask (my prob 0%, modal NO) — staying out.
current.rationale_summary: The base rate of US-Iran nuclear deals historically indicates a low likelihood of successful agreements due to mutual distrust and geopolitical tensions. Recent evidence suggests that while discussions are ongoing, there are significant challenges and no clear signs of compromise from either side. Trump's involvement and edits to potential agreements indicate continued US skepticism or hesitation,
key_drivers: Mutual distrust between the US and Iran
Domestic political pressures in the US
Trump's influence on the negotiation process
Lack of significant progress in Iran's nuclear program
Potential role of international actors in mediation
reference_classes: Historical US-Iran nuclear negotiations with mixed outcomes
Successful and failed international nuclear agreements
Geopolitical negotiations involving high levels of distrust

history.lean_note: I lean NO (my prob 12%) but it isn't +EV at the ask — no value bet (betting the other side would contradict my forecast).
history.rationale_summary: A tentative US-Iran MOU to launch 60-day nuclear talks is unsigned; a full nuclear deal (the resolution bar) is downstream with unresolved enrichment/sanctions issues — unlikely within 30 days.
key_drivers: MOU unsigned
deal downstream of 60-day talks
JCPOA took 3 months

history.lean_note: I lean NO (my prob 22%) but it isn't +EV at the ask — no value bet (betting the other side would contradict my forecast).
history.rationale_summary: Diplomatic momentum has improved (reports of a 'final agreed text' and officials voicing high confidence), but the actual nuclear terms required to resolve YES remain deferred to follow-on talks, and Trump has repeatedly claimed a deal 'imminent' without one materializing; a qualifying formal deal before the ~July 1 close is more likely than not to fail.
key_drivers: nuclear terms deferred to later talks
repeated false 'deal imminent' claims
Iran enrichment red line
new Supreme Leader hardening stance
18-day window

history.lean_note: I lean NO (my prob 5%) but it isn't +EV at the ask — no value bet (betting the other side would contradict my forecast).
history.rationale_summary: June 14-19 MOU is explicitly a roadmap, not a nuclear deal; opens a 60-day nuclear negotiation window ending ~mid-August — after the July 1 close. Talks contentious (missiles, rebuilding).
key_drivers: MOU is roadmap not deal
60-day window ends mid-Aug past close
contentious nuclear talks
reference_classes: comprehensive nuclear agreements concluded within weeks

history.lean_note: no +EV value bet at the ask (my prob 4%, modal NO) — staying out.
history.rationale_summary: June 17 Islamabad MOU is a war-ending ceasefire, not a nuclear deal; the June 21-22 Switzerland round produced only a 60-day roadmap (~late Aug) with enrichment and IAEA access still disputed as of June 23.
key_drivers: MOU not nuclear deal
60-day roadmap
enrichment unresolved

history.lean_note: no +EV value bet at the ask (my prob 0%, modal NO) — staying out.
history.rationale_summary: Recent evidence from multiple credible sources indicates significant progress in US-Iran talks, including agreement on a roadmap for a final deal, resumption of nuclear inspections, and temporary sanctions relief. However, historical challenges with implementation and verification, along with ongoing regional tensions such as the conflict in Lebanon, introduce uncertainty. The evidence suggests a
key_drivers: Recent progress in talks and agreement on a roadmap for a final deal
Resumption of nuclear inspections and temporary sanctions relief
Historical challenges with implementation and verification
Ongoing regional tensions
such as the conflict in Lebanon
reference_classes: Nuclear agreements between the US and Iran
Diplomatic negotiations involving sanctions and verification
